rationale: The cited paper focuses on hedonic–utilitarian discount framing in bundles, while the citing paper’s core contribution is about variety-seeking vs. inertia and immediate vs. delayed promotions, so substantive dependence is not obvious from the notes alone. There is some plausible overlap because the citing paper’s Study 3 explicitly controls for hedonic/utilitarian perceptions, which could indicate use of this literature. However, the matched evidence is only a bare DOI reference with no in-text citation context, so the pair cannot be safely dropped or confidently kept.
